summary: Stocks posted a second straight weekly gain amid a fragile US-Iran ceasefire, lifting risk appetite across equities, crypto, and bonds while driving gold to record highs on residual safe-haven demand. Friday's University of Michigan consumer sentiment index fell to 47.6, the lowest reading on record, a stark reminder that Main Street confidence has not followed Wall Street's relief rally.
tweet: Iran headline volatility drove markets all week, but signs of a de-escalation off-ramp snapped the S&P's 5-week losing streak — even as consumer sentiment collapsed to a record low.
